title: calceolaria floribunda
intro: Calceolaria floribunda, also known as Slipper Flower, is a flowering plant species native to South America, characterized by its unique bell-shaped flowers in shades of yellow, orange, and red. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Calceolaria floribunda include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antioxidant properties. Herbal preparations of the plant can be found in teas, infusions, and tinctures, often used to treat digestive issues, skin problems, and respiratory infections.
